questions.question: Where is the three-day ‘Rashtra Raksha Samarpan Parv’ held?
questions.explanation: The Defence Ministry’s celebration event named Rashtra Raksha Samarpan Parv’ is held at Jhansi as a part of the Azadi Ka Amrit Mahaotsav’ celebrations in India. Under this event, the Prime Minister would launch and dedicate multiple initiatives of the Defence sector to the nation and the event aims to provide thrust to ‘Make in India for Defence sector’.

questions.question: Checkmate, a new stealth aircraft seen in the news, is being developed by which country?
questions.explanation: Russia is developing its second stealth fighter jet after the twin-engine Su-57 Felon. ‘Su-75 Checkmate’, the new stealth aircraft is also known as the Light Tactical Aircraft (LTC). The aircraft has been developed by the United Aircraft Corporation, which is a part of state defense and technology conglomerate, Rostec. Checkmate is a fifth-generation light fighter jet, which will take its maiden flight in 2023.

questions.question: Which pharma company has signed license agreement with Medicines patent pool (MPP), for Covid Oral candidate?
questions.explanation: Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) is a United Nations-backed public health organization working to increase access to life-saving medicines for low- and middle-income countries. Pfizer Inc. and MPP signed a voluntary license agreement for Pfizer’s COVID-19 oral antiviral treatment candidate ‘PF-07321332’. The agreement will enable MPP to facilitate additional production and distribution of the antiviral and to provide greater access to the global population.

questions.question: Which place in India, was the East Asian bird species “Korean flycatcher” spotted recently?
questions.explanation: The yellow-rumped flycatcher also known as the Korean flycatcher or the tricolor flycatcher is a bird species found in East Asian countries like Mongolia, Transbaikal, southern China, Korea and Japan. Recently, the bird was spotted in Nanmangalam forest in Chennai, Tamil Nadu. Due to their stable population, the species has been classified as “Least Concern” as per IUCN.

questions.question: Which country inaugurated the first global innovation summit of the pharma sector?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the first global innovation summit of the pharma sector via video conferencing. The two-day event was attended by more than 40 national and international speakers covering a range of subjects such as funding for innovation, industry-academia collaboration, and innovation infrastructure.

questions.question: Which Indian state has been ranked first in the Indian Police Foundation (IPF)’s Smart Policing Index?
questions.explanation: The Indian Police Foundation (IPF) launched the Smart Policing Index 2021. IPF used six competency-based dimensions and three values-based dimensions of trust in the survey. It measures public perceptions and citizen satisfaction in the survey. The level of satisfaction with the quality of policing was highest in Andhra Pradesh (8.11), Telangana (8.10), Assam (7.89). They are followed by Kerala, Sikkim and Mizoram.

questions.question: Which state launched the ‘5T High School Transformation Programme’?
questions.explanation: Odisha launched 5T High School Transformation Programme, under which existing schools are being renovated with modern furniture and brighter lighting systems. Odisha Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik inaugurated more than 150 renovated government-run high schools. More than 1000 government schools are being refurbished across the state.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian constitution gives Parliament the power to make laws and repeal laws?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the three controversial farm laws passed last year would be repealed. For laws that do not have a sunset clause, Parliament passes another legislation to repeal the law. Article 245 of the Constitution gives Parliament the power to make laws for the whole or part of India, and state legislatures the power to make laws for the state. The same provision gives power to Parliament to repeal a law.

questions.question: Toliman mission, which was seen in the news recently, is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: The Toliman mission, will search for potential planets orbiting Alpha Centauri A and B. The Toliman telescope, which is under construction, is set to be launched into low-earth orbit in 2023. The mission has been named after the ancient Arabic-derived name for the star system. Alpha Centauri, Earth’s closest neighbouring star system, consists of two sun-like stars, known as Alpha Centauri A and B.

questions.question: Which country is set to host a summit of Foreign and Development Ministers from the G7?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom is set to host a summit of Foreign and Development Ministers from the G7 next month in the city of Liverpool. The UK is holding the G7 Presidency in 2021 and this is the second in-person meeting of G7 Foreign Ministers in 2021, following May’s meeting in London. Representatives from the US, France, Germany, Italy, Canada, Japan and the EU would participate.
